description: The batched _apply_work_dir_extra_paths has no test for stale symlink replacement (where a symlink exists at the target but points to the wrong source). Commit 304b7b2ac introduced ln -snf for this case, and the batched rewrite preserved the behavior in the shell script via the readlink -f comparison in the elif branch, but there is no unit test verifying this scenario works. A regression in this area would go undetected.

In the batched symlink creation shell script, the CONFLICT error message uses printf with the shell-quoted target path as the %s argument (e.g., printf 'CONFLICT: %s\n' '/work/.venv'). The shell will strip the outer quotes, so the printed path is unquoted. The Python parsing at line 1625 uses line.removeprefix('CONFLICT: ') which works correctly. However, the FAILED error messages at line 1618 use the same pattern. If there are both CONFLICT and FAILED errors for different paths, the Python error handling at lines 1625-1630 only reports the conflicts (UserInputError) and silently drops the FAILED messages. This means symlink creation failures are unreported when conflicts also occur.
